Reported by BBC World, CBS News, Al Jazeera and others

items.title: Trump booed in New York as he becomes first US president to attend NBA Finals
items.standfirst: The catcalls came after ticketholders faced airport-style security to enter the venue at Madison Square Garden.
items.content: The catcalls came after ticketholders faced airport-style security to enter the venue at Madison Square Garden.

items.title: President Trump booed by MSG crowd prior to Game 3 of NBA Finals
items.standfirst: Donald Trump was booed loudly by fans inside Madison Square Garden when he was shown on video screens during the national anthem prior to Game 3 of the NBA Finals on Monday.
items.content: Donald Trump was booed loudly by fans inside Madison Square Garden when he was shown on video screens during the national anthem prior to Game 3 of the NBA Finals on Monday.

items.title: 6/8: The Takeout with Major Garrett
items.standfirst: Iran and Israel say they've halted military operations after weekend flare-up; Trump attends NBA Finals game at Madison Square Garden.
items.content: Iran and Israel say they've halted military operations after weekend flare-up; Trump attends NBA Finals game at Madison Square Garden.

items.title: New York City crowd boos US President Donald Trump at NBA playoff game
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump was loudly booed when he was shown on screens before Game 3 of the NBA Finals.
items.content: US President Donald Trump was loudly booed when he was shown on screens before Game 3 of the NBA Finals.

items.title: Donald Trump given hostile reception as New York crowd boos and jeers president at NBA finals
items.standfirst: President attends Spurs v Knicks game at MSGKnicks aiming to win first title since 1973Game report: Wembanyama ruins Garden’s partyDonald Trump was loudly booed when he was shown on the video screens at Madison Square Garden on Monday night before Game 3 of the NBA finals between the San Antonio Spurs and New York Knicks.Trump was shown on the jumbotron while the Star-Spangled Banner was being sung before the game, and jeers and boos broke out around the arena. The president was shown for a little over eight seconds and held a salute the whole time with a smile on his face. A few seconds later, the video board showed Knicks players in line and the boos turned to cheers. Continue reading...
items.content: President attends Spurs v Knicks game at MSGKnicks aiming to win first title since 1973Game report: Wembanyama ruins Garden’s partyDonald Trump was loudly booed when he was shown on the video screens at Madison Square Garden on Monday night before Game 3 of the NBA finals between the San Antonio Spurs and New York Knicks.Trump was shown on the jumbotron while the Star-Spangled Banner was being sung before the game, and jeers and boos broke out around the arena. The president was shown for a little over eight seconds and held a salute the whole time with a smile on his face. A few seconds later, the video board showed Knicks players in line and the boos turned to cheers. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump NBA Finals visit locks down 5-block perimeter around Madison Square Garden
items.standfirst: President Trump will watch the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs in Game 3 of the NBA Finals from an executive suite, as an invited guest of Madison Square Garden CEO James Dolan. Ed O'Keefe reports.
items.content: President Trump will watch the New York Knicks and San Antonio Spurs in Game 3 of the NBA Finals from an executive suite, as an invited guest of Madison Square Garden CEO James Dolan. Ed O'Keefe reports.

items.title: Details on extreme security restrictions over Trump's NBA Finals visit
items.standfirst: President Trump will be in attendance Monday night for Game 3 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden. CBS News' Anna Schecter has details on the security surrounding the visit.
items.content: President Trump will be in attendance Monday night for Game 3 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden. CBS News' Anna Schecter has details on the security surrounding the visit.

items.title: Trump gets a cold reception at NBA Finals game as Spurs beat Knicks
items.standfirst: Victor Wembanyama helped the San Antonio Spurs reignite the NBA Finals on Monday with a win over the New York Knicks, while US President Donald Trump's appearance at Madison Square Garden drew boos from fans and prompted an extensive security operation around the arena.
items.content: Victor Wembanyama helped the San Antonio Spurs reignite the NBA Finals on Monday with a win over the New York Knicks, while US President Donald Trump's appearance at Madison Square Garden drew boos from fans and prompted an extensive security operation around the arena.

clusters.title: OpenAI confidentially files for US IPO as AI boom drives investor frenzy
clusters.neutral_headline: Tech giant OpenAI files for US initial public offering
bullet_summary: OpenAI did not disclose the size or terms of the offering and said a timeline has not yet been determined
The ChatGPT-maker announced it has filed paperwork to go public, just a week after rival Anthropic took the same step

items.title: OpenAI confidentially files for US IPO as AI boom drives investor frenzy
items.standfirst: ChatGPT maker OpenAI has confidentially filed for a US initial public offering, the company said on Monday, joining rivals such as Anthropic in a race towards public markets amid surging investor interest in artificial intelligence. The firm did not disclose terms or timing.
items.content: ChatGPT maker OpenAI has confidentially filed for a US initial public offering, the company said on Monday, joining rivals such as Anthropic in a race towards public markets amid surging investor interest in artificial intelligence. The firm did not disclose terms or timing.

items.title: OpenAI Files Confidentially for IPO
items.standfirst: OpenAI, the maker of ChatGPT, filed confidentially for an IPO, joining artificial intelligence rivals in tapping public markets to fund ambitious growth plans. Bloomberg's Michael Hytha has more. (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: OpenAI, the maker of ChatGPT, filed confidentially for an IPO, joining artificial intelligence rivals in tapping public markets to fund ambitious growth plans. Bloomberg's Michael Hytha has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Tech giant OpenAI files for US initial public offering
items.standfirst: OpenAI did not disclose the size or terms of the offering and said a timeline has not yet been determined.
items.content: OpenAI did not disclose the size or terms of the offering and said a timeline has not yet been determined.

items.title: OpenAI confidentially files for initial public offering on US stock market
items.standfirst: ChatGPT maker expected to be valued at more than $850bn, one of most highly valued listings in market historyOpenAI has filed confidentially to go public on the US stock market, according to a company blogpost published on Monday. The artificial intelligence giant’s debut on Wall Street is expected to be one of the most highly valued listings in market history with a valuation at more than $850bn.“We recently submitted a confidential S-1. We expect it to leak so we’re just announcing it,” the company’s post reads. “We have not decided on timing yet; it may be a while because there are things we want to do that are likely easier as a private company. But it’s a complicated set of tradeoffs and this gives us the option to go public sooner if that ends up being best.” Continue reading...
items.content: ChatGPT maker expected to be valued at more than $850bn, one of most highly valued listings in market historyOpenAI has filed confidentially to go public on the US stock market, according to a company blogpost published on Monday. The artificial intelligence giant’s debut on Wall Street is expected to be one of the most highly valued listings in market history with a valuation at more than $850bn.“We recently submitted a confidential S-1. We expect it to leak so we’re just announcing it,” the company’s post reads. “We have not decided on timing yet; it may be a while because there are things we want to do that are likely easier as a private company. But it’s a complicated set of tradeoffs and this gives us the option to go public sooner if that ends up being best.” Continue reading...

items.title: OpenAI Files Confidentially for IPO
items.standfirst: OpenAI filed confidentially for an IPO with the SEC, working with Goldman Sachs Group and Morgan Stanley on a potential listing. Romaine Bostick reports on "Balance of Power." (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: OpenAI filed confidentially for an IPO with the SEC, working with Goldman Sachs Group and Morgan Stanley on a potential listing. Romaine Bostick reports on "Balance of Power." (Source: Bloomberg)

clusters.title: Powerful earthquake in southern Philippines leaves at least 37 dead
clusters.neutral_headline: Powerful earthquake in southern Philippines leaves at least 37 dead
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, CBS News, BBC World and others

items.title: Powerful earthquake in southern Philippines leaves at least 37 dead
items.standfirst: People told not to enter damaged buildings for fear of aftershocks from magnitude-7.8 quakeAt least 37 people have died and hundreds have been injured after a magnitude-7.8 earthquake shook part of the southern Philippines early on Monday, collapsing buildings and triggering tsunami alerts.The quake hit early in the morning about 20km (12.4 miles) off the coast of Sarangani province, with tremors felt strongly across Mindanao and 420km away in the city of Manado on the Indonesian island of Sulawesi. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

clusters.title: Israel and Iran step back from further strikes after renewed clashes
clusters.neutral_headline: Has Trump lost control of the Iran war
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Israel and Iran step back from further strikes after renewed clashes
items.standfirst: Israel and Iran appeared to pull back from further military action on Monday after exchanging fire for the first time since a US-brokered ceasefire two months ago. Both sides warned they would retaliate if attacked again, while regional powers and the Trump administration pushed to prevent the conflict from escalating into a wider war.
items.content: Israel and Iran appeared to pull back from further military action on Monday after exchanging fire for the first time since a US-brokered ceasefire two months ago. Both sides warned they would retaliate if attacked again, while regional powers and the Trump administration pushed to prevent the conflict from escalating into a wider war.

items.title: Has Trump lost control of the Iran war?
items.standfirst: For the first time since the start of a precarious ceasefire two months ago, Israel and Iran have traded missile strikes.
items.content: For the first time since the start of a precarious ceasefire two months ago, Israel and Iran have traded missile strikes.

items.title: Iran war: who is fighting and why?
items.standfirst: Arch-enemies Israel and Iran have returned to active confrontation while Donald Trump tries to present himself as mediatorIsrael and Iran have returned to active war for the first time since a ceasefire was agreed two months ago in an exchange of rocket fire that threatened efforts to end the conflict.Donald Trump, who started the war in February alongside Israel but has since attempted to present himself as a mediator, told the two sides to stop shooting and said “final negotiations” on peace were proceeding. By late afternoon on Monday, the attacks had stopped. Continue reading...
items.content: Arch-enemies Israel and Iran have returned to active confrontation while Donald Trump tries to present himself as mediatorIsrael and Iran have returned to active war for the first time since a ceasefire was agreed two months ago in an exchange of rocket fire that threatened efforts to end the conflict.Donald Trump, who started the war in February alongside Israel but has since attempted to present himself as a mediator, told the two sides to stop shooting and said “final negotiations” on peace were proceeding. By late afternoon on Monday, the attacks had stopped. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Raman defeats Pratt to face Bass in LA mayoral election, CBS News projects
clusters.neutral_headline: Nithya Raman edges out Spencer Pratt to face Karen Bass in LA mayoral runoff
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Guardian World

items.title: Raman defeats Pratt to face Bass in LA mayoral election, CBS News projects
items.standfirst: CBS News projects that incumbent Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass will face off against Nithya Raman in November's runoff election.
items.content: CBS News projects that incumbent Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass will face off against Nithya Raman in November's runoff election.

items.title: Nithya Raman edges out Spencer Pratt to face Karen Bass in LA mayoral runoff
items.standfirst: Progressive challenger to face incumbent mayor in November as former reality star Pratt trails behindNithya Raman, a progressive Los Angeles city council member, has advanced to the November runoff for LA mayor, edging out former reality TV villain Spencer Pratt for the chance to face incumbent mayor Karen Bass.Pratt, who decided to run for mayor after his Pacific Palisades home burned down in the 2025 wildfires, held a lead over Raman for days. But as ballot processing from last week’s election continued, the city councillor pulled ahead. Continue reading...
items.content: Progressive challenger to face incumbent mayor in November as former reality star Pratt trails behindNithya Raman, a progressive Los Angeles city council member, has advanced to the November runoff for LA mayor, edging out former reality TV villain Spencer Pratt for the chance to face incumbent mayor Karen Bass.Pratt, who decided to run for mayor after his Pacific Palisades home burned down in the 2025 wildfires, held a lead over Raman for days. But as ballot processing from last week’s election continued, the city councillor pulled ahead. Continue reading...

items.title: ICC chief prosecutor Karim Khan suspended amid sexual misconduct inquiry
items.standfirst: Khan, a prominent British lawyer, has repeatedly denied the allegations which first emerged in 2024The chief prosecutor of the international criminal court, Karim Khan, has been suspended after a disciplinary process triggered by sexual abuse allegations against him reached a conclusion.The ICC’s governing body announced the decision on Monday evening after its executive committee voted to refer the proceedings against Khan to a special session of the court’s member states for them to consider his future. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Trump storms out of NBC interview after denying his own “No War” promises
clusters.neutral_headline: Trump storms out of NBC interview after denying his own “No War” promises

items.title: Trump storms out of NBC interview after denying his own “No War” promises
items.standfirst: Donald Trump stormed off from his interview for NBC’s “Meet the Press," after he was fact-checked on his false claims about election fraud by reporter Kristen Welker. She also asked him about the Iran war, his controversial "anti-weaponization" fund and the cost of living, though Trump made a serious of false and exaggerated responses. He also denied having made campaign "promises" about starting any wars before walking out entirely.
items.content: Donald Trump stormed off from his interview for NBC’s “Meet the Press," after he was fact-checked on his false claims about election fraud by reporter Kristen Welker. She also asked him about the Iran war, his controversial "anti-weaponization" fund and the cost of living, though Trump made a serious of false and exaggerated responses. He also denied having made campaign "promises" about starting any wars before walking out entirely.

items.title: Read the transcript: President Donald Trump interviewed by NBC News’ ‘Meet the Press’ moderator Kristen Welker
items.standfirst: Trump spoke about the war with Iran, the “anti-weaponization” fund, the economy, recent elections in California and more in June 2026 interview.
items.content: Trump spoke about the war with Iran, the “anti-weaponization” fund, the economy, recent elections in California and more in June 2026 interview.

items.title: Fact-checking Trump’s interview with NBC News’ ‘Meet the Press’
items.standfirst: Digging into some of the president’s claims about the Iran war, the Jan. 6 riots, California’s primary elections and more.
items.content: Digging into some of the president’s claims about the Iran war, the Jan. 6 riots, California’s primary elections and more.

clusters.title: Russian interference in Armenia: A nation tested by disinformation
clusters.neutral_headline: Armenia's pro-West government wins election despite Russian pressure

items.title: Russian interference in Armenia: A nation tested by disinformation
items.standfirst: Under the leadership of Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan, Armenia is moving to distance itself from Russia and forge closer ties with the European Union. Despite pro-Russian disinformation campaigns targeting the government, the pro-European candidate won the parliamentary elections held on June 7.
items.content: Under the leadership of Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan, Armenia is moving to distance itself from Russia and forge closer ties with the European Union. Despite pro-Russian disinformation campaigns targeting the government, the pro-European candidate won the parliamentary elections held on June 7.

items.title: Armenia's pro-West government wins election despite Russian pressure

items.content: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's Civil Contract Party secures nearly 50% of the vote, comfortably beating the other contenders.
